meta.title: Lore × Leviathan Bridge Report
meta.description: Full cross-repo report covering the CDO, capture, proposal, and execution slices for the Lore-Leviathan bridge.

props.title: Lore × Leviathan bridge through the real ready boundary
props.subtitle: CDO → capture → proposal → exec was carried across both repos until the remaining blocker became contract richness rather than seam discovery.

props.content: This report is the state of the bridge as it actually exists on disk. Lore now owns a pluggable simulation seam, a thin Lev consumer adapter, and a display-name provenance lift. Leviathan now owns a bounded SDK roster emitter with focused regression coverage. The unresolved work is no longer 'where should the seam live?' but 'how much richer should the contract become before it stops being a thin projection and starts becoming canonical bridge truth?'

props.label: STATUS
props.title: Current Readiness
props.subtitle: What actually shipped versus what remains intentionally deferred

props.label: ANSWER
props.title: Decision That Survived CDO
props.subtitle: The bridge is layered, bounded, and intentionally asymmetric

props.title: Keep Lev thin, let Lore own the first richness step
props.content: Lev should stop at the verified DNA SDK emitter. Lore should own the first materialization step from `DnaAgentRef[]` into non-canonical sim-safe bridge records.

**Why this held up:** every CDO pass converged on the same pattern. Lore already had the semantic and simulation nucleus, so rewriting it into Lev would have been fake progress. Lev already had the correct DNA/compiler seam, so pushing Lore bridge richness back into Lev would have widened the wrong layer. The result is a deliberately asymmetric bridge: Lev emits the thinnest verified source, Lore performs the first non-canonical enrichment, and richer provenance stays deferred until a contract audit closes the gap.

props.label: OWNERSHIP
props.title: Who Owns What Now
props.subtitle: The current split is explicit rather than implied

props.title: Lore Owns Now
props.content: Simulation semantics, tick/world loop, field normalization, non-canonical bridge defaults, thin Lev consumer adapter, and display-name hydration from referenced DNA.

props.title: Leviathan Owns Now
props.content: Thin verified roster emission at the DNA SDK seam, compile/validate plumbing, and the future decision surface for whether richer roster semantics should exist at all.

props.label: TIMELINE
props.title: Execution Timeline
props.subtitle: The work only advanced when the next slice was genuinely ready

items.title: 1. Cross-repo overlap CDO
items.description: Result: layered merge, not rewrite. The bridge was narrowed to a Lev heartbeat/runtime shell around Lore simulation semantics.

items.title: 2. Lore-local seam
items.description: The simulator seam was made pluggable and normalized without touching tick/event control flow.

items.title: 3. Lev roster emitter
items.description: A pure `emitVerifiedRoster()` helper shipped in `plugins/dna`, validated by focused tests and a real DNA fixture.

items.title: 4. Thin consumer adapter
items.description: Lore added a non-canonical adapter that shells out to the verified Lev emitter and maps the result into sim-safe defaults.

items.title: 5. Display-name provenance lift
items.description: The adapter now dereferences `ref.dna` read-only and hydrates authored display name with soft-fail fallback.

items.title: 6. Contract audit
items.description: Decide whether richer provenance stays local, whether per-field provenance labels are needed, and whether a richer upstream roster contract should ever exist.

props.label: ARTIFACTS
props.title: Artifact Matrix
props.subtitle: What exists on disk right now

rows.meaning: Minimum compatibility contract with explicit non-canonical boundary
Pure SDK roster helper plus focused tests
Thin non-canonical adapter over the verified Lev emitter

props.label: CURRENT SHAPE
props.title: Live Roster Output and Bridge Reality
props.subtitle: The emitted shape is real, but still thin

props.title: What the real fixture proved
props.content: The compliance analyst DNA fixture now emits a verified roster through the Lev SDK. That roster is real enough for execution, but still too thin to count as faithful canonical bridge truth.

props.label: BLOCKERS
props.title: What Is Still Intentionally Deferred
props.subtitle: These are no longer seam-finding problems; they are contract and provenance problems

props.title: Canonical Bridge Truth
props.content: The current adapter output is explicitly non-canonical. The system still lacks faithful cross-repo `bridge_entity[]` truth.

props.title: Per-Field Provenance
props.content: There is still no per-field provenance labeling. Only whole-record non-canonical status exists today.

props.title: Richness Policy
props.content: The next decision is policy, not plumbing: which richer fields stay local forever, and which should eventually move upstream.

props.label: NEXT
props.title: Recommended Next Move
props.subtitle: What to do if you want to keep going without reopening settled ground

props.content: **Do next:** run a contract audit focused on provenance richness. The practical question is no longer whether the seam exists. It does. The real question is whether richer provenance should stay a Lore-owned read-only projection, or whether a future Lev contract should emit more than `DnaAgentRef[]`. Until that decision is made, the current adapter should stay thin, explicit, and non-canonical.

rows.step: Keep using the current adapter for experiments
rows.reason: The adapter is validated and respects the current boundary.
Would start creating richer semantic truth before the contract audit.

rows.step: Promote adapter output to canonical bridge truth
rows.reason: Per-field provenance and richer contract semantics are unresolved.
